picks.narrative: IBP makes PIT 47.5% fair versus a 40.1% no-vig market price — a 7.5pp gap. The model isn't leaning on a marquee matchup here — no single baseball factor rises to a headline; the disagreement is with the posted price itself. The main pushback is pitcher quality (xfip) (~-0.9pp): Bubba Chandler 4.71 xFIP vs Max Fried 3.79 xFIP. The market has drifted against the pick since posting, widening the available price — the current +150 still sits inside the playable range (to +130). This isn't a clean all-signal agreement — it's the model calling the posted number too generous for the true win probability.

negative_drivers.detail: Bubba Chandler 4.71 xFIP vs Max Fried 3.79 xFIP.

negative_drivers.detail: NYY defense grades +9 OAA better on the season — a run-prevention headwind.

negative_drivers.detail: The opposing starter is the better-rested side.

negative_drivers.detail: NYY's home-field context weighs against the pick playing on the road.

picks.narrative: The model sees OAK as a 52.1% side; the market is pricing 47.7%, leaving a 4.8pp edge. The largest lift is pitcher quality (xfip) (~+1.8pp): Gage Jump 3.93 xFIP vs Merrill Kelly 5.85 xFIP. The main pushback is defense (oaa) (~-0.6pp): The model's defensive-efficiency adjustment leans to the opponent. The market has drifted against the pick since posting, widening the available price — the current +111 still sits inside the playable range (to +108). The path is exploiting the ARI weakness the model flags before they can stabilize.

picks.narrative: IBP values MIL 4.4pp higher than the market — 61.6% model vs 56.8% implied. The largest lift is home field (~+1.0pp): Home-park and 2026 home-game context add a modest edge for MIL. The price has held roughly steady since posting — the current -134 still sits inside the playable range (to -136). The edge is the model pricing MIL differently than the market rather than one of the marquee starter/lineup/bullpen buckets — worth taking at the number, not chasing past it.

picks.narrative: The model prices TEX at 54.3% to win where the no-vig market implies 49.8%, a 4.3pp disagreement. Several smaller signals stack the same way — pitcher quality (xfip) (~+1.2pp), home field (~+1.1pp) — with no single one dominating. The price has held roughly steady since posting — the current +100 still sits inside the playable range (to -101). The case is a stack of small same-direction signals, not one dominant mismatch — treat it as the thinner edge it is.

The main pushback is defense (oaa) (~-0.6pp): KC defense grades +15 OAA better on the season — a run-prevention headwind. The price has held roughly steady since posting — the current -114 still sits inside the playable range (to -115). The win condition is pressuring KC's soft spot before they can settle in.
